name: Grade 1 Beginner Coding — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for algorithms, sequences, loops, and responsible device use. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in algorithms, sequences, loops, and responsible device use.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore algorithms, sequences, loops, and responsible device use
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record algorithms, sequences, loops, and responsible device use by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

resources.title: CSTA K–12 Computer Science Standards
resources.description: Planning reference for this Grade 1 Beginner Coding pathway.

units.title: Investigate: algorithms, sequences, loops, and responsible device use
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about algorithms, sequences, loops, and responsible device use?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record algorithms, sequences, loops, and responsible device use.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore algorithms, sequences, loops, and responsible device use
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record algorithms, sequences, loops, and responsible device use by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record algorithms, sequences, loops, and responsible device use. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to algorithms, sequences, loops, and responsible device use.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
